original_hypothesis.title: Manager Drains Reserved Withdrawal Assets via manage() Arbitrary Call
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The manage() function allows the manager to execute arbitrary calls from the vault, including calling underlyingToken.transfer() to drain all assets including those reserved for fulfilled withdrawal requests (reservedWithdrawalAssets). No check ensures the vault retains sufficient underlying token balance to honor fulfilled withdrawals. Users who have burned shares for withdrawal requests would permanently lose their claims.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While the manager is a trusted role, the manage() function provides capability to break the core solvency invariant (reserved assets backing) with a single call. No guard-rails prevent draining reserved assets even accidentally.

proof_of_signal.pattern: manage() calls target.functionCallWithValue(data, value) with no restriction on target being underlyingToken or amount exceeding free balance

preconditions: Manager is compromised or acts maliciously

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: manage() has zero restrictions on targets or amounts; can transfer underlying token including reserved amounts
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Manager can unilaterally drain all assets
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Anytime manage() is callable with reservedWithdrawalAssets > 0

operational_questions: Is the manager a multisig or EOA?
Are there off-chain monitoring systems for vault balance vs reserved assets?
Could manage() be restricted to disallow direct underlying token transfers?
validation_requirements: Call manage() with underlying token transfer exceeding free balance
Verify executeWithdrawal reverts when vault balance < assetsToWithdraw

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Compromised manager calls manage(underlyingToken, transfer(attacker, fullBalance), 0)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All underlying tokens drained including reservedWithdrawalAssets
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Users call executeWithdrawal — reverts on insufficient balance
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Users have already burned shares; no recovery path

potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Requires off-chain asset recovery; users' shares already burned

execution_requirements_analysis.technical_skills: Basic — construct manage() call with token transfer

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Large outbound token transfers from vault via manage()

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
